name: Gwalchmai
description: Gwalchmai, also known as Gawain in Arthurian romances, is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology and Arthurian legend. He is often depicted as a courteous, brave, and skilled knight, embodying chivalric ideals. Gwalchmai is the son of King Lot of Orkney and Morgause (Anna in Welsh tradition), making him the nephew of King Arthur. He is renowned for his strength, which waxes and wanes with the sun; he is strongest at midday. Gwalchmai is a warrior of great renown, known for his prowess in battle and his loyalty to Arthur. While often portrayed as honorable and virtuous, some Welsh tales hint at a more complex character, capable of ruthlessness when necessary. He is a key member of Arthur's court, participating in many quests and battles. His death, often attributed to wounds sustained in battle against Mordred, marks a significant loss for Arthur and his kingdom. Gwalchmai represents the ideals of heroism, loyalty, and martial skill, though his character is not without its shades of grey, reflecting the complexities of human nature even within the realm of myth.
subtitle: The Knight Whose Strength Grew With the Sun
achievements: Defeating the Giant of the Whirlpool
Rescuing the Princess of the Silver Palace
Slaying the Dragon of Mount Snowdon

keyMyths.title: Gwalchmai and the Giant of the Whirlpool
keyMyths.description: Gwalchmai bravely confronts and defeats a monstrous giant dwelling in a dangerous whirlpool, saving countless lives and proving his exceptional courage. This tale highlights his strength and selfless dedication to protecting the innocent.

keyMyths.title: Gwalchmai's Strength and the Sun
keyMyths.description: Gwalchmai's strength increases with the rising sun, reaching its peak at midday before gradually waning as the sun sets. This unique characteristic makes him an incredibly powerful warrior during daylight hours.

keyMyths.title: Gwalchmai's Loyalty to Arthur
keyMyths.description: Gwalchmai remains fiercely loyal to King Arthur, fighting alongside him in numerous battles and upholding the values of the Round Table. His unwavering devotion is a cornerstone of his heroic character.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A collection of early Welsh prose stories, including tales featuring Gwalchmai.
sources.text: A collection of early Welsh prose stories, including tales featuring Gwalchmai.

sources.author: Geoffrey of Monmouth
sources.description: A pseudo-historical account of British history that helped popularize the Arthurian legend.
sources.text: A pseudo-historical account of British history that helped popularize the Arthurian legend.
